plan_type_rationale: Three sequenced bug fixes in the rag-index code path, each with its own test scenario. Multi-phase but no policy gates -> phased.

blast_radius: rag-index CLI exit-code semantics (consumed by coderef-pipeline orchestrator)
SQLite vector store on-disk layout (potentially user data)
public_api_hits: rag-index CLI exit codes (semver-meaningful for CI consumers)
potentially: SQLite store path (.coderef/rag-vectors.sqlite is now a file vs the directory it currently is)
hotspot_hits: chunk-converter EISDIR errors on LLOYD (Windows path handling)
warnings: Phase 2 changes the on-disk layout of .coderef/rag-vectors.sqlite. Existing users will need a one-time --reset or manual rm. Documented in plan + closeout.

goal: Fix three rag-index defects (vector store --reset bug, exit-code conflation, Python chunk-conversion failure) caught during WO-UNIFIED-CODEREF-PIPELINE-001 P6 LLOYD verification.

in_scope: src/cli/rag-index.ts: exit-code semantics for empty/skipped/failed indexing
src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts: EISDIR fix + Python chunker coverage
out_of_scope: RAG search ranking, query handling, or other rag-search.ts behavior
RAG provider/LLM wiring (covered by WO-UNIFIED-CODEREF-PIPELINE-001 Phase 4)
Foundation-doc generation (covered by Phase 2 of the predecessor WO)
constraints: Must not regress the local-only RAG enforcement shipped in WO-UNIFIED-CODEREF-PIPELINE-001 Phase 4 + Phase 6 (CODEREF_RAG_LOCAL_ONLY guard).
rag-index direct CLI usage (without the orchestrator) must remain back-compat for users not setting CODEREF_RAG_LOCAL_ONLY.

source_followups: FOLLOW-UP-3 from WO-UNIFIED-CODEREF-PIPELINE-001 P6-VERIFICATION.md (Python chunk skip)
